title: paspalum distichum var. littorale
intro: Paspalum distichum var. littorale, also known as Littoral Paspalum or Beach Grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Littoral Paspalum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to address various health issues, such as urinary problems and skin conditions.
